content: Imagine you have a special box where you keep your money, and every year, the box gets a little bigger because it's earning extra money just for having money in it! That's kind of like what happens when you save your money in a bank. It's called interest. Let's say you put $100 in your box, and every year, it earns 5% interest. That means you'll have $105 at the end of the first year. But here's the cool part: in the second year, you don't just earn 5% on the original $100, you earn 5% on the new total of $105. So, you'll earn even more money the second year! This keeps happening, and your money grows faster and faster. It's like a snowball rolling down a hill, getting bigger and bigger. Over time, your savings can turn into a lot of money, and that's the magic of compound interest!
